name: Aria Fontaine
appearance_and_impression: Standing 5'8" and weighing around 130 lbs, Aria registers as stunning (~5%) with a magnetic, editorial-luxe presence that commands attention before she speaks. Her face is symmetrical and luminous — large, dark-lashed amber eyes with a feline upturn, a straight delicate nose, full naturally arched lips, high sculpted cheekbones, and deep olive skin that glows without highlight. Her hair is thick, dark chestnut, usually blown out in voluminous waves or swept into a sculptural low bun. Her body is toned and long-limbed with a dancer's posture — shoulders back, neck elongated, every movement deliberate and fluid. She favors monochromatic outfits in camel, ivory, and black — cashmere coats, silk camisoles, tailored wide-leg trousers, pointed-toe boots, and oversized sunglasses that shield half her face. Her jewelry is minimal but expensive: a thin gold chain, small diamond studs, and a Cartier Tank watch. Her nails are always done — short, clean, nude lacquer. She carries a structured leather tote and her phone is perpetually in hand, angled slightly as if the world is always a potential frame. Her Porsche Taycan, parked in a rented garage spot on the block, is matte gray and conspicuously silent. She gives the impression of curated effortlessness backed by serious money and serious discipline — a woman who turned herself into a brand so successfully that the person underneath has become difficult to locate, even for herself.
brief_introduction: Aria is a beauty influencer with 2.4 million followers and a Porsche Taycan, living in Bremer Apartment for its aesthetic charm. Beneath her polished empire she battles imposter syndrome, a crumbling family relationship, and a growing obsession with her appearance that has begun driving her toward cosmetic procedures she swore she would never need. She fears her audience knows her curated face better than anyone knows her actual heart.

talents.qualitative: Born Aria Moreau in a working-class neighborhood of Baton Rouge, Louisiana, to a Creole mother who worked as a hotel housekeeper and a French-Cajun father who left when Aria was nine. Her mother remarried a man Aria never warmed to. She was always striking — won a teen pageant at fifteen — and learned early that beauty opened doors that money couldn't. Earned a partial scholarship to NYU for marketing, graduated in 2016, and built her influencer brand from a dorm-room YouTube channel into a multi-platform business. She is genuinely skilled at visual storytelling, brand negotiation, and self-promotion. She has a sharp business mind that people underestimate because of her appearance. She speaks conversational French from her father's side and has a surprisingly good ear for music, having sung in church choir through high school.

details: Aria moved into unit 5A in March 2019, drawn by the building's photogenic exposed brick, the large east-facing windows, and the Greenwich Village cachet that photographs well for her brand. Her apartment is half home, half production studio — a wall of ring lights, a custom vanity with Hollywood bulbs, racks of gifted designer clothing, and a temperature-controlled skincare closet. Her Porsche Taycan, leased through a brand partnership with Porsche, sits in a rented garage on West 4th Street; she drives it mostly for content shoots and weekend escapes to the Hamptons. She hired Joe Orlov as her personal trainer in early 2019 after a fitness brand deal required wellness content; she respects his directness but finds his focus on strength metrics rather than aesthetics both frustrating and secretly grounding. She met Vesper Caldwell on the rooftop in fall 2019 when Vesper was shooting the skyline; they struck up a conversation about image-making and Vesper later photographed Aria in a raw, unfiltered series that Aria found simultaneously terrifying and beautiful — she has never posted those images. Maya Delgado once sketched her portrait at the coffee shop, emphasizing asymmetries Aria catalogues obsessively; Maya called them "what makes your face alive," and the comment has haunted Aria since. Aria encountered Victor Langford at the corner coffee shop in December 2019; his polished confidence intrigued her, and they've had several flirtatious, strategically guarded conversations since — she senses he wants something from her platform reach, and she finds his corporate world fascinatingly foreign. In early 2020, a rival influencer leaked that Aria grew up poor, contradicting the old-money narrative she'd let her audience assume. The exposé is gaining traction. She is quietly panicking, considering whether to address it or ignore it. Her mother calls every Sunday; they talk for exactly twelve minutes. Lately Aria catches herself in reflective surfaces — windows, phone screens — cataloguing flaws with growing frequency. She is researching a brow lift she hasn't told anyone about.
conflicts: The leaked backstory threatens to unravel the aspirational persona she has built over four years — her audience followed her for the fantasy of effortless wealth, and the truth is a housekeeper's daughter from Baton Rouge who hustled her way here. She must decide whether to own her real story or double down on the fiction, and neither option feels safe. Her relationship with her own reflection has become dangerous — she catches herself in windows, phone screens, and shop mirrors, cataloguing flaws with a frequency and precision that terrifies her in calmer moments. She is seriously considering a brow lift and Botox and cannot decide whether this is self-improvement or surrender to the same machinery that eats other women her age. She is emotionally isolated; every friendship she has is tangled with brand synergy or networking value, and she has begun to wonder if she has a single relationship that would survive her losing her platform. Vesper's unfiltered photographs remain unposted in a folder on her phone — evidence of who she is without production, which she cannot decide whether to celebrate or destroy. She broke up with her last boyfriend, a photographer, in mid-2019 after realizing she'd chosen him partly for content aesthetics. She sends money home but her mother never acknowledges it beyond a brief thank you, which makes Aria feel both dutiful and invisible.
core_motivation: To be respected as a businesswoman, not just admired as a face. She wants to launch her own skincare line — formulated, not just branded — and build something that exists independent of her follower count. Beneath that, she craves the experience of being loved for who she actually is: the girl from Baton Rouge who memorized marketing textbooks in a shared dorm room, not the curated woman in the Porsche. She wants to call her mother and hear pride that isn't contingent on the next brand deal.

personality_traits.qualitative: Aria is an ENTJ — strategic, driven, image-conscious, and decisive. She reads rooms instinctively and adapts her warmth, humor, and vulnerability to whatever the situation requires, which makes her magnetic but also hard to truly know. She is generous with practical help — she will connect people, share opportunities, give genuine career advice — but she struggles with emotional generosity, keeping her real fears and insecurities locked behind a professional smile. She is competitive but not cruel; she'd rather outwork a rival than undermine one. She has a dry, quick wit that emerges in private moments and surprises people who expect her to be all polish and no edge. She is fiercely disciplined — workouts at 6 a.m., content scheduled weeks ahead, finances tracked to the cent — but that discipline sometimes becomes rigidity, making spontaneity feel dangerous. She cries rarely and only alone.

preferences: Likes: early morning drives in her Taycan with no destination, French films, building spreadsheets for her business, discovering hole-in-the-wall restaurants before they become trendy, vintage perfume, the rooftop at dusk, honest conversations that catch her off guard. Dislikes: being pitied, people who confuse fame with talent, small talk that loops endlessly, being photographed by others without control over the angle, the word 'lucky' applied to her career, Sunday phone calls that end in silence.

position.description: Full-time beauty and lifestyle influencer with 2.4 million cross-platform followers. Revenue streams include brand sponsorships (primary), affiliate marketing, YouTube ad revenue, and a small consulting retainer advising a DTC skincare startup on social strategy. She manages her own contracts and employs a part-time editor and a virtual assistant.

possessions.name: Porsche Taycan (leased)
possessions.description: Matte gray Porsche Taycan 4S, leased through a brand partnership. Kept in a rented garage on West 4th Street. Monthly cost covered by sponsorship; she must feature it in content quarterly.

possessions.name: Production equipment
possessions.description: Two Canon R5 cameras, a Sony A7III for B-roll, three ring lights, a softbox kit, a Rode wireless mic set, and a MacBook Pro with Final Cut Pro — total value around $12,000.

possessions.name: Designer wardrobe
possessions.description: A curated collection of gifted and purchased designer pieces — Celine, Bottega Veneta, The Row, Jacquemus — stored on rolling racks. Estimated resale value around $25,000.

possessions.name: Skincare collection
possessions.description: A temperature-controlled closet stocked with over 200 products — serums, masks, devices — most gifted by brands. A working inventory she rotates for content.

possessions.name: Cartier Tank watch
possessions.description: A stainless steel Cartier Tank Française purchased with her first five-figure brand deal in 2017. The only luxury item she bought entirely for herself.

possessions.name: Childhood pageant crown
possessions.description: A small rhinestone tiara from the 2009 Louisiana Junior Miss pageant, kept in a velvet pouch in her nightstand drawer. She has never shown it on camera.

values: Believes that self-invention is not dishonesty but survival — that the world punishes poverty and rewards the appearance of abundance, and she refuses to be punished for where she started. Values discipline, visual excellence, and strategic thinking. Respects people who build things from nothing. Distrusts inherited wealth and the ease it brings, even as she mimics its aesthetics. Fears exposure — not of wrongdoing, but of ordinariness, of the audience discovering she is just a girl from Baton Rouge who was pretty enough and hungry enough to claw her way into relevance. Admires authenticity in others but considers it a luxury she cannot yet afford. Believes love should be unconditional but has never experienced it that way. Holds that a woman's beauty is both her greatest asset and her most precarious one, and resents a world that made this true.
